mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust labor demand and consumer income expansion. This drives near-term equity valuations upward through increased earnings expectations and reduced recession risk, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with labor market stabilization
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market healing
failure_condition: Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains elevated above 5% while inflation-adjusted real rates are restrictive. This sustained monetary tightness depresses equity valuations by raising the cost of capital and reducing present value of future cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% and real rate (FEDFUNDS - CPI YoY change) > 2.5%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite elevated real rates, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × real_rate_proxy) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline month-over-month, indicating weakening construction demand and reduced forward-looking consumer confidence. This signals deteriorating aggregate demand momentum, causing equity investors to reduce growth expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1238.0 thousand units in Jan 2010, post-crisis recovery phase
regime_hint: post-crisis demand recovery with housing sector fragility
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 5% are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC of HOUST month-over-month change versus 60-day returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates, reflecting expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum. This signals improving corporate profitability and operational efficiency, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.89 in Jan 2010, recovering from 2009 trough
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO month-over-month change versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO surprises fail to produce positive returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates while unemployment remains elevated above 4%. This joint condition signals weakening consumer purchasing power and demand destruction. Equity investors reduce earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in Feb 2010, RSAFS at 709668M in Jan 2010
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with persistent labor slack and demand weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances despite weak retail sales and elevated unemployment, or the interaction term (RSAFS_growth × UNRATE) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling accelerating labor market deterioration and rising recession risk. Equity investors flee growth assets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices tail risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.2% in Feb 2010, elevated from 2007 baseline
regime_hint: labor market deterioration phase with recession risk
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC of UNRATE 3-month change versus 60-day returns is below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains below 2.5% annualized while the federal funds rate stays above 5%, creating a regime of monetary tightness relative to growth. This policy-growth mismatch signals central bank error and future policy reversal expectations, but near-term equity valuations compress due to current restrictive conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22924.9B in Q4 2009, FEDFUNDS at 5.33% in Feb 2010
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with weak growth backdrop
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite weak growth and tight monetary conditions, or the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × inverse_GDPC1_growth) shows Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth accelerates month-over-month while housing starts expand, signaling synchronized demand momentum across consumer and construction sectors. This joint expansion signals robust aggregate demand and forward earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month growth > 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:Post-2009 recovery phase with synchronized demand signals
regime_hint: post-crisis demand recovery with synchronized sector expansion
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following synchronized retail and housing strength are negative in > 50% of instances, or the interaction term (RSAFS_growth × HOUST_growth) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window.
